name: Strangler migration

problemSignals.description: A wholesale replacement would combine behavior discovery, migration, and cutover risk.
Traffic or responsibilities can be routed through explicit bounded seams.

requiredEvidence.description: Observed legacy inputs, outputs, side effects, and failure semantics at candidate seams.
Traffic allocation, observability, fallback, and state-consistency mechanisms.

assumptions: A migration seam can be isolated
Old and new paths can coexist under explicit routing and data-consistency rules

deterministicSteps.action: Bound one responsibility and document its legacy behavior, dependencies, and rollback route.
Define new-path contracts, routing fraction, state handling, observability, and rollback thresholds.
Compare both paths under the declared contract and produce retirement evidence or an unresolved gap list.

outputs.description: Bounded legacy responsibility and interface contract.
Parity, rollback, and dependency-removal evidence; not an adoption decision.

whenAll: Legacy behavior is covered by contracts
Long-tail behavior or state migration remains partially observed
Rollback or dual-run consistency cannot be established

contraindications: There is no isolatable routing or responsibility seam
Dual operation creates unacceptable safety or consistency risk

contract: Preserve rollback and per-path observations without automatic cutover

outOfScope: Systems with inseparable state and behavior
